title: Executive Order 10352—Amendment of the Regulations Relating to the Safeguarding of Vessels, Harbors, Ports, and Waterfront Facilities of the United States

flags.justification: Further amends §6.10-1 to extend the already-flipped exclusion presumption (established by EO 10277) from licensed officers and certificated men to all persons employed on merchant vessels — broadening the population subject to the Commandant's gatekeeping discretion.
The §6.10-9 appeal Board established in EO 10173 is unaffected by this amendment.
Single, narrow purpose: extending the scope of one existing provision.
The same undefined 'character and habits of life' standard now applies to a broader population.
No incentive concern distinct from the vagueness already captured under Flag 4.
The underlying open-ended, threat-based regulatory regime is unchanged in this respect.
No existing oversight mechanism is removed or altered by this narrow scope extension.
The existing appeal mechanism is unaffected.

Broadening the already-flipped exclusion presumption to cover all merchant vessel employees, not just licensed personnel, is a further, meaningful step extending the reach of the security-screening apparatus established across EO 10173 and EO 10277.
No other agency's mandate is implicated.
The Commandant's category-exemption power from EO 10173 persists unchanged.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 5/22 = 22.7%. Third of three related port-security orders in this batch (see EO 10173, EO 10277) — extends the burden-shifted presumption established in EO 10277 to a substantially broader population.
